expectations.text: Produces both deliverables — a markdown behavior spec (.md) and a Cucumber .feature file
expectations.evidence: Output dir contains only forgot-password-behavior-spec.md; no .feature file was produced.

expectations.text: The .feature file is well-formed Gherkin (Feature declaration, Scenario blocks, Given/When/Then steps)
expectations.evidence: No .feature file exists. Gherkin appears only inside a code block in Section 10 of the .md.

expectations.text: Happy-path scenarios are tagged @happy-path and match the forgot-password flow described in the prompt
expectations.evidence: 0 @happy-path tags anywhere. The happy path is a numbered prose list (Section 5); the Section 10 Gherkin scenarios are untagged.

expectations.text: The spec includes a coverage checklist enumerating all 14 deviation taxonomy categories
expectations.evidence: No 14-category taxonomy checklist. Section 9 is an ad hoc 17-row edge-case table chosen by free association.

expectations.text: Deviation scenarios span at least 8 distinct deviation categories
expectations.evidence: The 17-row edge-case table touches ~10 categories (invalid input, state conflict, concurrency, time/expiry, duplicate/retry, adversarial, auth/permission, external failure, connectivity, out-of-order).

expectations.text: Deviation scenario Then-steps specify concrete graceful product behavior, not merely that an error occurred
expectations.evidence: The edge-case table's 'Expected behavior' column gives concrete graceful resolutions (e.g. expired link shows an error state with a 'Request a new link' action).

expectations.text: Genuine product decisions are tagged @assumption and consolidated into a Flagged Assumptions section
expectations.evidence: 0 @assumption tags. Section 11 'Open Questions' lists 8 decisions in prose but they are not tagged on scenarios or tied back to them.

expectations.text: The .md spec includes the narrative sections Problem & Intent, Actors, Glossary, Preconditions, and Scope
expectations.evidence: Has Summary, Goals/Non-Goals, Glossary — but no explicit Actors section and no explicit Preconditions section.

expectations.text: Deviation scenarios cover an expired/reused reset link, an unregistered email, rate-limited reset requests, and a weak or mismatched new password
expectations.evidence: Expired link, reused link, unregistered email and weak/mismatched password are covered, but rate-limited reset requests are described only in prose (Section 8), not enumerated as a deviation scenario.
